questions.text: The principle of mathematical induction is used to prove statements about:
questions.explanation: Mathematical induction is used to prove statements P(n) for all natural numbers n.

questions.text: In mathematical induction, we first prove the base case P(1).
questions.explanation: In mathematical induction, we first prove P(1) is true (base case), then assume P(k) and prove P(k+1).

questions.text: The sum of first n natural numbers is ________.
questions.explanation: 1 + 2 + 3 + ... + n = n(n+1)/2. This can be proved using mathematical induction.

questions.correctAnswer: Base: n=1, LHS=1, RHS=1. Assume true for k. For k+1: k² + (2k+1) = (k+1)²
questions.explanation: By induction: Base case n=1: 1 = 1² ✓. Assume true for k. For k+1: Sum = k² + (2k+1) = (k+1)² ✓
